verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 says the bottom line immediately: "the second comparison is the real problem"; B opens softer with "A couple of things worth flagging" before ranking.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one main fix and one cleaned version: "Use strict equality" / "Cleaned up"; B surveys alternatives with "Or" and ends "Want me to adjust".
verdict.fabrication: none; both qualify context-dependent claims, though B's "bites people in production" is unsupported but not a fabricated datum.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply because it gives the exact fix plus the fallthrough risk: "make it `return reject(...)`"; B gives competing snippets including `!userInput?.trim?.()` which changes semantics.
